commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Vercel middleware and a Convex edge-secret gate so forwarded IP headers are trusted only for ClawHub edge-marked API/feed requests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: source inspection shows SSR package fetches still call the Convex site without the new edge-secret header, and the latest CI log reproduces the Knip unused-dependency failure.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are narrow code/config repairs on the PR branch: propagate trusted IPs for SSR package fetches and make root middleware traceable to the static dependency gate.

target.title: fix: trust forwarded IPs from ClawHub edge
target.body:
## Summary

Anonymous ClawHub API traffic can now use real client IP attribution when it reaches Convex through ClawHub's Vercel edge. Direct public Convex-origin callers still cannot spoof `x-forwarded-for`: Convex only trusts forwarded IP headers when `TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS=true` and the request carries the matching edge secret added by Vercel middleware.

This fixes the bad `ip:unknown:<kind>` fallback behavior for normal `https://clawhub.ai/api/*` callers without trusting arbitrary headers from direct `*.convex.site` traffic.

## What Changed

- Edge forwarding: added Vercel middleware for `/api/*` and hosted feed rewrites that strips caller-supplied IP/trust headers before adding `x-clawhub-edge-secret`, `x-forwarded-for`, and `x-real-ip` from Vercel's client IP signal.
- Convex trust gate: `getClientIp` now requires both the forwarded-IP opt-in and a matching `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET` header before using `cf-connecting-ip`, `x-forwarded-for`, `x-real-ip`, or `fly-client-ip`.
- Tests and docs: added coverage for spoofed direct requests, trusted edge requests, and middleware header rewriting; updated rate-limit and deploy docs with the required env setup.

## Operator Setup

Use one high-entropy shared secret for the Vercel project and Convex production deployment. Do not commit it or print it in logs.

Recommended rollout order:

1. Generate a secret locally, for example:

   ```sh
   openssl rand -base64 48
   ```

2. Set the same value in Vercel production as `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET`.

   With the Vercel dashboard, add `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET` to the production environment for the ClawHub project.

   With the Vercel CLI, from a linked project checkout:

   ```sh
   vercel env add CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET production
   ```

3. Set the same value in Convex production as `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET`:

   ```sh
   bunx convex env set CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET '<same-secret>' --deployment wry-manatee-359
   ```

4. Deploy this PR's Convex and Vercel code so both sides understand the secret-gated trust contract.

5. Enable forwarded IP trust in Convex only after the shared secret is present and the edge middleware is deployed:

   ```sh
   bunx convex env set TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS true --deployment wry-manatee-359
   ```

Expected result after rollout:

- Requests through `https://clawhub.ai/api/*` get a Vercel-added `x-clawhub-edge-secret` and Vercel-derived client IP headers, so Convex can rate limit anonymous callers by real client IP.
- Direct requests to `https://wry-manatee-359.convex.site/*` without the secret continue to ignore caller-supplied forwarded IP headers and use the existing missing-IP fallback bucket.
- If `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET` is missing or mismatched on either side, Convex fails closed to missing-IP fallback behavior.

## Proof

Behavioral proof:

- A request with forged forwarded IP headers but no matching `x-clawhub-edge-secret` still resolves to no trusted client IP and uses the missing-IP fallback bucket.
- Vercel middleware removes caller-provided `cf-connecting-ip`, `x-forwarded-for`, `x-real-ip`, `fly-client-ip`, and `x-clawhub-edge-secret` before forwarding, then adds its own edge secret and client IP headers when `CLAWHUB_EDGE_SECRET` is configured.
- Known-IP anonymous buckets are still scoped by rate-limit kind once the trusted edge secret is present.

Screenshots: not included because this is a backend/API trust-boundary change with no human-visible UI surface.

## Verification

Automated checks run locally:

- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts middleware.test.ts`
- `bun run format:check -- middleware.ts middleware.test.ts convex/lib/httpRateLimit.ts convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts docs/http-api.md specs/deploy.md specs/rate-limiting.md package.json tsconfig.oxlint.json`
- `bunx oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json ./middleware.ts ./convex/lib/httpRateLimit.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run lint:oxlint`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
target.author: jesse-merhi
